diseases.name: Fibrous Dysplasia
diseases.summary: Somatic GNAS mutations replace normal bone with fibrous tissue. Disease often progresses in childhood and usually stabilises in adulthood.

guidelinePromptProfile.clinicalFraming: Fibrous dysplasia (FD) is a mosaic GNAS-related disorder of bone. Distinguish monostotic, polyostotic, and craniofacial FD. MAS overlap may include café-au-lait macules and endocrinopathies — do not collapse FD and MAS into one undifferentiated entity.
guidelinePromptProfile.pubmedRetrieval: Prioritise FD/MAS bone disease, GNAS mutations, fracture risk, pain, craniofacial complications, and medical therapy (bisphosphonates, denosumab where reported). Include McCune-Albright only when explicitly on FD–MAS spectrum.
guidelinePromptProfile.synthesisEmphasis: Emphasise skeletal staging, surgical vs medical management, malignant transformation surveillance, endocrine screening when MAS features suspected, and pregnancy/bone pain pathways.

diseases.name: McCune-Albright Syndrome
diseases.summary: Classic triad: fibrous dysplasia of bone, café-au-lait macules, and endocrinopathies (most often precocious puberty). Spectrum overlaps with FD.

guidelinePromptProfile.clinicalFraming: McCune-Albright syndrome (MAS) is a mosaic GNAS disorder with fibrous dysplasia of bone, café-au-lait skin pigmentation, and endocrinopathies (classically precocious puberty). Treat endocrine, skeletal, and dermatologic domains explicitly.
guidelinePromptProfile.pubmedRetrieval: Search MAS, McCune-Albright, GNAS mosaicism, precocious puberty, fibrous dysplasia bone disease, and peripheral precocious puberty management. Separate from Noonan/RASopathy literature.
guidelinePromptProfile.synthesisEmphasis: Cover endocrine surveillance (growth, thyroid, adrenal, GH excess), bone protection, skin pigmentation counselling, and fertility/reproductive endocrinology where evidence exists.

diseases.name: Noonan Syndrome
diseases.summary: Congenital heart disease (often pulmonary valve stenosis), short stature, characteristic facies, and lymphatic or bleeding disorders.

guidelinePromptProfile.clinicalFraming: Noonan syndrome is a RAS-MAPK pathway disorder with variable expressivity: congenital heart disease (often pulmonary valve stenosis), short stature, distinctive facies, bleeding/lymphatic issues, and developmental needs. Gene may be PTPN11, SOS1, RAF1, KRAS, RIT1, or others.
guidelinePromptProfile.pubmedRetrieval: Use Noonan syndrome and RASopathy terms; include pulmonary stenosis, hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, growth hormone, bleeding diathesis, and malignancy surveillance (JMML) where relevant. Exclude McCune-Albright/fibrous dysplasia unless comorbidity is studied.
guidelinePromptProfile.synthesisEmphasis: Structure around cardiac surveillance, growth/endocrine management, haematology/bleeding, developmental support, cancer surveillance protocols, and pregnancy counselling.
homonymsToAvoid: LEOPARD as generic label without context

content_prs.title: Update: denosumab dosing schedule for pediatric polyostotic FD
content_prs.author: AI Watcher
content_prs.summary: Based on four recent articles (2024–2025): extend maintenance intervals from every 4 weeks to every 12 weeks after induction when CTX response is sustained.

diff.text: Denosumab 60 mg subcutaneously every 4 weeks until skeletal maturity.
Denosumab 60 mg subcutaneously every 4 weeks for six induction doses, then every 12 weeks if CTX remains below 0.3 ng/mL.
Monitor calcium and 25-OH vitamin D weekly during induction, then monthly during maintenance.

content_prs.title: Add: optic canal monitoring protocol for asymptomatic patients
content_prs.author: AI Watcher
content_prs.summary: Add serial orbital MRI every 12 months for asymptomatic patients with imaging-evident optic canal involvement.

diff.text: For asymptomatic patients with imaging-evident optic canal involvement: MRI orbits with contrast every 12 months.

papers.title: The Natural History of Fibrous Dysplasia of the Orbit
Cystic Degeneration of Craniofacial Fibrous Dysplasia

content_prs.title: Clarify: when to refer for genetic testing vs histology alone
content_prs.author: AI Watcher
content_prs.summary: Bone scintigraphy only at initial mapping; routine follow-up scans not recommended.

diff.text: Bone scintigraphy: initial mapping only. Routine follow-up scintigraphy is not recommended (cumulative radiation, low yield).

content_prs.title: Endocrine surveillance intervals in MAS
content_prs.author: AI Watcher
content_prs.summary: Shorten Tanner staging interval to every 6 months in MAS girls aged 2–7 years.

diff.text: Annual Tanner staging in MAS girls aged 2–8.
Tanner staging every 6 months in MAS girls aged 2–7 (peak window for GnRH-independent precocious puberty).

content_prs.title: Cardiology follow-up after pulmonary valve intervention
content_prs.author: AI Watcher
content_prs.summary: Echo follow-up every 6 months for 24 months after balloon valvuloplasty or surgery.

diff.text: Post-intervention echocardiography at 1, 6, 12, and 24 months, then annually if stable.
